# ESN Document And Slide Taxonomy

This taxonomy captures reusable document families for consulting and integration work. It is intentionally brand-neutral.

## Document Families

| Family | Finality | Core sections |
|---|---|---|
| Mission proposal | Sell and frame a scoped engagement | Context, objectives, approach, team, planning, assumptions, price frame, next steps |
| Delivery report | Align stakeholders during execution | Status, decisions, risks, delivery evidence, next milestones, asks |
| Assessment report | Explain findings and recommendations | Scope, method, findings, impact, options, roadmap, appendix |
| Steering memo | Prepare a decision committee | Decision needed, options, tradeoffs, recommendation, consequences |

## Slide Families

| Family | Use when | Expected content |
|---|---|---|
| Cover | Start any formal deck | Client, topic, date, confidentiality, owner |
| Agenda | Set the meeting contract | 3 to 5 sections maximum |
| Situation | Establish shared context | Facts, constraints, business signal |
| Problem framing | Explain why action is needed | Symptoms, root causes, impact |
| Target state | Describe what good looks like | Outcomes, operating model, principles |
| Option comparison | Support a choice | 2 to 3 options, criteria, tradeoffs |
| Recommendation | State the proposed path | Decision, rationale, expected benefit |
| Roadmap | Sequence work | Phases, milestones, dependencies |
| Team and governance | Make delivery credible | Roles, cadence, responsibilities |
| Commercial frame | Explain effort and assumptions | Scope, exclusions, pricing model placeholder |
| Next steps | Close with action | Decisions, owners, dates |

## Content Guardrails

- A slide family should be reusable across Scalian/CGI-like ESN decks without carrying their visual identity.
- The structure should survive a theme swap: Sentropic, DSFR, Carbon, Airbus or a client theme only changes presentation.
- Appendices carry proof; main-flow slides carry decisions.
